basics.name: Arik Joukhajian
basics.label: Microbial Ecologist & PhD Researcher
basics.summary: PhD researcher in microbial ecology specializing in mycorrhizal fungi and post-fire microbiome dynamics.

education.score: Minor in Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences

work.name: UCR, Glassman Lab
highlights: Conducted field work in the Mojave Desert for microbial ecology assessments
Analyzed and presented next-generation sequencing data
Trained undergraduate students in applied lab techniques

work.name: UCR, Gachomo Lab (Rotation)
highlights: Tested methods of fungal DNA extraction from plant roots
Amplification of target fungal sequences for analysis

work.name: UCR, Stajich Lab (Rotation)
highlights: Assessment of lichenized fungal genomes for enzyme activity
Analysis of secondary metabolite profiles from fungi

work.name: Fulgent Genetics
highlights: Performed RNA extraction for high-throughput COVID-19 testing
Maintained workflow by assisting with all aspects of sample processing

work.name: VRL Eurofins Pre-Transplant Screening
highlights: Increased sample throughput by streamlining workflow of automated testing
Resolved client issues in a timely and professional manner

work.name: UCLA, Research Immersion Laboratory for Microbiology
highlights: Cultured and sequenced bacterial isolates with anti-fungal compound production
Developed research project from budgeting stage to poster presentation

work.name: UCLA, Tina Treude Lab
highlights: Sample-processing of deep-sea sediment core samples
Assisted with sample collection aboard the RV Yellowfin in the Santa Monica Bay

work.name: USC, Center for Dark Energy Biosphere Investigations
highlights: Produced bacterial qPCR data for 40+ deep sea sample sites
Maintained and prepared new enrichments of microbial samples

publications.name: Eastern Joshua Tree Arbuscular Mycorrhizal Fungi Largely Consistent Across Roots, Soils, and Seasons
Mojave Desert microbial communities show high resistance and resilience over three years despite widespread plant mortality following the Dome Fire
publications.summary: Joukhajian A, Pulido Barriga MF, Davis MJ, Sweet LC, and Glassman SI

publications.name: Effects of Long-Term Fire Retardants on Mycorrhizal Colonization and Soil Microbiomes
publications.summary: Quaal RJ, Enright DJ, Joukhajian A, Zhilik BB, and Glassman SI

publications.name: Uncovering Arbuscular Mycorrhizal Fungi and Microbiome Responses to Dryland Fires
publications.summary: PhD Dissertation, University of California, Riverside

awards.title: Microbiology Award for Excellence in Mycology
Underground Explorer Grant (Lead Researcher)
awards.awarder: Society for the Protection of Underground Networks (SPUN)

volunteer.summary: Co-founded ICAM, a 501(c)(3) non-profit to foster international collaboration and support mycology research in Armenia
Reviewing research submissions for local publications
Judged student research projects for the Riverside Unified School District
